role: Scholar of ancient Iranian literature; Tehran University professor; assassinated 1997
bio: Ahmad Tafazzoli was a prominent scholar of ancient Iranian literature and a professor at Tehran University. On 15 January 1997 he was found dead in a suburb of Tehran — his skull and bones had been crushed, and his body was stuffed into the trunk of his car. His murder was later attributed to the same [Ministry of Intelligence](/orgs/ministry-of-intelligence) unit responsible for the broader [chain murders](/events/chain-murders-iran-1998) campaign. Tafazzoli's killing was one of the most brutal of the earlier (pre-1998) chain murders, which only came to light after the high-profile assassinations of late 1998.
editorial_notes: Born 1937. Ancient Iranian literature scholar, Tehran University. Found dead 15 January 1997, skull crushed, body in trunk of car.
